name: Holiday Fruitcake with Louisiana Pecans
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine raisins, currants, citron, and pecans in a large bowl.
2. In a separate bowl, sift together flour, nutmeg, mace, and cinnamon.
3. Add the fruit mixture to the sifted flour mixture and mix well.
4. Cream butter until soft and smooth, then add sugar thoroughly.
5. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each egg is added.
6. Mix in orange marmalade, honey, and sherry.
7. Stir in the remaining fruit and nut mixture, ensuring it's evenly distributed throughout the batter.
8. Pour the batter into greased loaf tins lined with waxed paper, two thicknesses thick.
9. Top with candied cherry halves and whole pecans halved.
10. Bake at 250°F for 2-1/2 to 3 hours, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
11. Allow cakes to cool completely before wrapping and storing them in a cool place. They will improve in flavor after 2 or 3 weeks of aging.
